full_text_rationale: Full text describes MEMCoder using multi-dimensional evolving task-level and API-level memory that is updated from execution feedback and reused for private-library-oriented code generation on NdonnxEval and NumbaEval.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 011; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Mofei Li, Taozhi Chen, Guowei Yang, Jia Li
titulo: MEMCoder: Multi-dimensional Evolving Memory for Private-Library-Oriented Code Generation
formato_armazenamento: Multi-dimensional Evolving Memory with task-level entries storing requirements, generated code, execution feedback, used APIs, and cross-API usage guidelines; API-level entries storing API documentation, code snippets, execution outcomes, dynamic usage guidelines, and guideline weights.
framework_agente: MEMCoder private-library-oriented code-generation framework with guideline-driven generation and feedback-driven memory evolution
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; RAG baseline comparison; continual-learning baseline comparison; ablation; token-cost analysis
benchmarks_utilizados: NdonnxEval and NumbaEval private-library code-generation benchmarks
metricas_desempenho: Pass@1, Pass@3, Pass@5, Exec@1, Exec@3, Exec@5
metricas_cl: Task-level memory ablation, API-level memory ablation, naive accumulation ablation, guideline extraction/refinement, dynamic guideline weight update, long-term memory bloat discussion
baseline_comparado: Naive RAG, EpiGen, CAPIR, ReMem, DC-RS, MEMCoder ablations, and oracle static documentation analysis
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory grows sequentially from generated code, execution feedback, task-level usage guidelines, API-level usage guidelines, code snippets, and weights; task-level retrieval uses similar historical tasks and API-level retrieval uses candidate API memories.
custo_por_tarefa: On NumbaEval, MEMCoder consumes an average of 17,111 additional total tokens per task, with estimated extra cost of about USD 0.46 for the whole 187-task benchmark.
resultado_principal: MEMCoder yields an average absolute Pass@1 gain of 16.31% when integrated into static RAG baselines and outperforms existing memory-based continual-learning baselines.
desempenho_vs_baseline: With Qwen2.5-Coder, Naive RAG plus MEMCoder reaches 52.54 Pass@1 on NdonnxEval and 33.74 on NumbaEval versus Naive RAG at 27.22 and 23.16. In continual-learning comparison, MEMCoder reaches 33.74 Pass@1 on NumbaEval versus ReMem 23.16 and DC-RS 17.33.
custo_vs_baseline: MEMCoder adds token overhead for memory injection and guideline refinement compared with naive RAG, but the paper estimates the added full-NumbEval cost as about USD 0.46.
limitacoes: Additional memory injection and reflection introduce token overhead; long-term memory growth may cause retrieval inefficiency and knowledge conflicts; benchmark scope is private-library generation on NdonnxEval and NumbaEval.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on local open-source models, bge-base-en-prior.5 retrieval, task ordering, memory-guideline quality, execution feedback reliability, private-library benchmark representativeness, and memory conflict management.
